topic.what: User-centric multi-agent system for detecting privacy inconsistencies between Android app permissions and Data Safety declarations
topic.how: Two-agent architecture using RAG-enhanced LLM to compare runtime-granted permissions against developer-disclosed data practices
topic.why_matters: Android privacy mechanisms rely on unverified developer declarations leaving users unaware of actual data collection practices causing 84% apps to have inconsistencies

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of apps with mismatches between granted permissions and Data Safety declarations
characteristics.context: Only 376 apps (16.02%) fully matched permissions with declarations

characteristics.what_it_measures: Agreement between LLM-based detection and manual expert analysis
characteristics.evidence: Four master students independently analyzed 25 apps each
characteristics.context: 10% false positives due to multi-role app complexity

characteristics.what_it_measures: Rate of incorrectly flagged inconsistencies without RAG
characteristics.evidence: Ablation study comparing RAG vs non-RAG performance
characteristics.context: RAG reduces false positives by 71% (from 35% to 10%)

characteristics.what_it_measures: On-device agent CPU consumption over 24 hours
characteristics.evidence: Android Studio Profiler measurement under real conditions
characteristics.context: Agent-1 runs as foreground service for continuous monitoring

characteristics.evidence: Android Studio Profiler measurement under real conditions
characteristics.context: Minimal overhead suitable for practical deployment

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of users willing to cancel installation after receiving warning
characteristics.context: 100% found tool useful (59.6% very useful 40.4% helpful)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average time for Agent-2 to generate warning and explanation
characteristics.context: Includes RAG retrieval LLM inference and summarization

characteristics.what_itasures: Breakdown of inconsistency types among inconsistent apps
characteristics.evidence: 751 Case1 (granted not declared) 563 Case2 (declared no permission) 657 Case3 (both)
characteristics.context: Case1: permission granted but not declared in DS Case2: DS declared but no permission granted Case3: both cases

good_for: explaining technical privacy risks to non-expert users

novelty: first user-centric multi-agent framework for on-device privacy inconsistency detection
RAG-enhanced LLM reasoning with external database of 14 sensitive data categories
client-server architecture with resource-constrained on-device agent
real-time permission monitoring vs static manifest analysis
summarization module for non-technical user explanations
open_problems: embedding LLM directly on Android device to eliminate off-device data transmission
cross-validation with multiple independent evaluators for agreement measurement
dynamic permission mechanism adapting to individual user preferences
handling multi-role apps where LLM misclassifies functional relevance
